title: strychnos pubiflora
intro: Strychnos pubiflora, a tropical plant native to Central and West Africa, is a member of the Loganiaceae family and is also known as the 'Yellow Stembark'. The medicinal properties of Strychnos pubiflora lie in its roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. Its roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antihelmintic properties, offering several health benefits such as treating fever, diarrhea, and intestinal parasites. Strychnos pubiflora is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture in herbal medicine, and it's also used in traditional remedies for its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various diseases.
